# Session Primer

## What This Project Does
A production weekly pipeline that processes Culex mosquito surveillance data (qPCR, trap counts, pool test results) for West Nile Virus and St. Louis Encephalitis Virus monitoring in Boulder County / Fort Collins, Colorado, and outputs a formatted Excel report plus CSVs and plots for public health epidemiologists.

## How to Run It
```bash
# 1. Generate config (once per week, sets all parameters)
bash config/run_config.sh --week 37 --year 2025 --download T --update T --push T

# 2. Render the pipeline
quarto render wnv-s_weekly_report_pipeline_v2.qmd
```

## Key Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| [`wnv-s_weekly_report_pipeline_v2.qmd`](../wnv-s_weekly_report_pipeline_v2.qmd) | Main pipeline — ~30 sequential Quarto chunks |
| [`config/config_weekly.R`](../config/config_weekly.R) | All parameters: week, year, thresholds, zone defs, GSheet keys |
| [`config/run_config.sh`](../config/run_config.sh) | Shell wrapper — run this before the QMD |
| [`utils/`](../utils/) | 36 utility functions (read/clean/calc/plot/merge) |
| [`1_input/wXX/`](../1_input/) | Raw weekly inputs: `datasheet/`, `pcr/`, `platemap/`, `all_species/` |
| [`2_mid/`](../2_mid/) | Intermediate `.RData` checkpoints (named `y{year}_w{week}_*`) |
| [`3_output/`](../3_output/) | Final Excel report, CSVs, and PNGs |
| [`config/load_packages.R`](../config/load_packages.R) | All `library()` calls — load before any function sourcing |

## How the Pieces Fit Together
`config/run_config.sh` calls `config_weekly.R` which saves all parameters to a timestamped `.RData` snapshot in `config/config_weekly_settings/`. The QMD loads that snapshot at the top, then proceeds linearly: download Google Sheets → read and clean PCR/platemap/datasheet/species files → merge into pool-level records → update the master Google Sheet → calculate abundance/PIR/Virus Index → reshape for historical comparisons → generate plots → export Excel report and CSVs. Each major intermediate is saved to `2_mid/` so individual steps can be re-run without reprocessing everything.

## Common Gotchas

- **Config must be run first.** If `config/config_weekly_settings/` has no `.RData` matching the current week, the pipeline will load the wrong parameters silently.
- **`PooledInfRate` is not on CRAN.** Install with `devtools::install_github("CDCgov/PooledInfRate")`. Without it, `calc_pir()` fails.
- **Google auth.** `googlesheets4` and `googledrive` require an interactive OAuth token on first use. Run once interactively; the token caches in `~/.config/gargle/`.
- **`--download F` for offline work.** If Google Drive is unavailable or you want to re-run on existing inputs, pass `--download F` to skip the download step.
- **Zone factor levels matter.** All zone columns must be coerced to the factor defined in `zone_lvls` (config). Skipping `wnv_s_clean()` will cause silent grouping errors in `calc_pir()` and `calc_vi()`.
- **`0_R/` is legacy.** Scripts there are exploratory or superseded. Active code is in `utils/` only.
- **No formal test suite.** The only informal test is `0_R/pir_test.R`. Manual validation is the current QA process.
